job_description:
About the Role

2Base Technologies is seeking a Marketing & Growth Lead to build and own its marketing function end-to-end, including strategy, team management, and results. As a technology services company with over 16 years of delivery experience, we operate primarily in the United States and the United Kingdom.

What You Will Own

  • Qualified lead targets and pipeline contribution from marketing
  • Team direction, weekly priorities, and ideal customer profile definition
  • Channel strategy, budget prioritization (inbound/outbound), and budget allocation
  • Marketing analytics, attribution, hiring, and team-building
  • Outbound marketing strategy, email nurture strategy, and marketing/sales alignment
  • Performance reporting to leadership

What You Will Not Own

  • Writing content, designing assets, or running paid campaigns directly. This is a leadership role focused on setting direction, building teams, and owning outcomes.

Requirements

  • Experience: 8–15 years in marketing (minimum 3–4 years in a senior/strategic role). Must have personally owned lead targets, managed teams, and operated in technology services, SaaS, or AI companies.
  • Skills: Inbound/outbound marketing, marketing analytics, US/UK B2B markets, cross-functional teamwork (sales/business development).
  • Education: MBA in Marketing or Business Administration preferred. Certifications in digital/growth marketing or analytics are advantageous.
  • Mindset: Outcome-driven, self-directed, decision-making with incomplete information, transparency in challenges, and team-building focus.

What We Are Not Looking For

  • Candidates measuring success via impressions, follower growth, or content volume without pipeline ties.
  • Experience in large teams with unclear ownership.
  • Tool-first approaches without problem understanding.
  • Lack of exposure to US/UK B2B buyers.

Benefits

  • Growth-driven environment with opportunities to lead, innovate, and upskill.

Contact: Share your resume at careers@2basetechnologies.com.
summary: Marketing & Growth Lead to build and own marketing function for a tech services company, requiring tech industry experience and leadership.

job_description:
Organic Growth & SEO Specialist

2Base Technologies is building its marketing function from a clear brief, and this role owns the organic search engine that sits at the centre of it. We are a technology services company with over 15 years of delivery experience, working with mid-market and enterprise clients across the United States and the United Kingdom. Our work spans AI implementation, custom software development, legacy system modernisation, and operational transformation.

What This Role Exists to Do

This role does not optimise content after it is written. It determines what gets written, why, and how it is structured so that the right people find it at the right moment in their decision process. The organic search function at 2Base is being built to generate qualified inbound pipeline from two specific markets, the US and UK, at scale. That means a large volume of high-intent pages, a technical foundation that performs, and a programmatic architecture that compounds over time. The person who fills this role designs that system and owns its results.

What You Will Own

  • The organic traffic strategy for the US and UK markets, including keyword targeting and the full architecture of how search drives inbound pipeline
  • Keyword research and search intent mapping across all service lines and target audiences
  • Page architecture of the website, including what pages exist, what they target, how they are structured, and how they connect through internal linking
  • On-page SEO across all published pages
  • Technical SEO oversight, working directly with the development team on crawlability, indexing, site speed, Core Web Vitals, and structured data
  • Google Search Console as a working instrument, including weekly review, issue resolution, and opportunity identification
  • Programmatic SEO build, including designing the framework and templates that allow the site to scale to 80 to 120+ high-intent pages
  • SEO briefs written clearly enough that the Content Specialist can execute without guessing
  • Link building target approval. The outreach team identifies candidates, and you approve or reject based on defined quality standards
  • SEO performance reporting, including organic traffic, keyword movement, pages published, and Search Console health

What You Will Not Own

  • Writing content: you brief the Content Specialist, you do not write the pages
  • Link building outreach: a dedicated team member executes this, you set the standard
  • Paid search: that is a separate role
  • Social media distribution: separate channel, separate owner
  • Content calendar scheduling: the Marketing & Growth Lead sets distribution priorities

Requirements

Experience - Minimum 3 to 5 years in an SEO or organic growth role, with strong candidates typically at the 4 to 6 year mark - Experience ranking high-intent commercial pages, not just informational content - Experience managing technical SEO independently - Experience building or contributing to a programmatic SEO architecture - Strong working knowledge of Google Search Console as an active tool - Experience working across US and/or UK markets - Working knowledge of AEO, GEO, and AIO, including how to structure content for AI-powered search surfaces - Ability to write SEO briefs that a content writer can execute without needing clarification

Mindset - Thinks in systems, not tasks. The goal is a compounding organic engine, not a checklist - Comfortable with ambiguity in early-stage environments - Analytically honest, able to diagnose what is not working and adjust direction independently - Collaborative but not passive. Works closely with content and development teams while driving the agenda

What We Are Not Looking For

  • SEO practitioners whose entire experience is informational content with no connection to commercial intent or pipeline
  • Anyone who treats Search Console as a monthly reporting task
  • Candidates who cannot explain the difference between high-intent and informational keyword targeting
  • Candidates whose SEO process depends entirely on a tool telling them what to do next
  • Candidates with no exposure to US or UK search behaviour who treat all markets as equivalent

Benefits

A growth-driven environment with opportunities to lead, innovate, and upskill continuously.

If this opportunity aligns with your career goals, kindly share your updated resume with us at careers@2basetechnologies.com.
summary: Strategic SEO leader building scalable organic search systems for US/UK markets. Owns keyword research, page architecture, and technical SEO to drive qualified inbound pipeline.

job_description:
Sr. Visual Designer 2Base Technologies

2Base Technologies is building its marketing function from scratch, and this role makes it look like a company worth taking seriously. We are a technology services company with over 15 years of delivery experience, working with mid-market and enterprise clients across the United States and the United Kingdom. Our work spans AI implementation, custom software development, legacy system modernisation, and operational transformation.

What This Role Exists to Do

Before a CTO reads a word of our content, they see the design. Before they trust the argument, they have already made a judgment about whether this company looks credible. This role owns that first impression, and then owns every asset that follows.

The visual function at 2Base is being built from the ground up. There is no brand system to inherit, no template library to use, and no consistent visual identity to maintain. What exists is a clear strategic direction, a defined audience of senior technology and operations leaders, and a marketing team building the content engine around it. Your job is to give that engine a visual identity it deserves.

The first 30 days of this role have one priority: build the brand system. Everything else, including LinkedIn carousels, ad creatives, landing pages, and presentation templates, is built from that foundation. A designer who wants to jump straight into production before the system exists is not the right fit.

This is not a creative agency role. The audience is sceptical, senior, and technically literate. Design that looks clever but reads as unclear, or feels consumer-facing rather than professional, will not work with this ICP. Restraint, precision, and consistency matter more than visual novelty.

What You Will Own

  • The 2Base visual brand system, including colour palette, typography, spacing, layout rules, and the documentation that makes it usable by the whole team
  • All marketing and content visual assets, including LinkedIn carousels, single-image posts, social graphics, infographics, and diagrams
  • Paid advertising creatives, including Google Display and LinkedIn Ads, designed to be tested and iterated
  • Landing page visual design, including layout, components, and conversion-focused structure, handed to the development team for build
  • Email visual templates, presentation templates, and sales deck templates
  • A template library that enables other team members to produce on-brand assets independently
  • Design consistency auditing, including periodic review of all published assets to ensure alignment with the brand system
  • Secondary responsibility: light video editing and motion for social reels, YouTube, and website use, supporting other team members

What You Will Not Own

  • What an asset communicates. The Content and Thought Leadership Specialist owns the message, argument, and information hierarchy. You may suggest improvements in structure, but content decisions remain with them
  • Copywriting. The Content Specialist writes the words; you structure them visually
  • Website development. You design the pages; the development team builds them
  • Content strategy. You execute what is briefed, not decide what gets created
  • Brand positioning. The Marketing Operating System defines how 2Base communicates; you interpret it visually

Requirements

  • Experience: Minimum 3 to 5 years in a B2B design role, preferably in technology, SaaS, or professional services
  • Experience building a brand system or significantly evolving one, not just producing isolated assets
  • Experience designing for LinkedIn, including carousel formats, document posts, and single-image feed content
  • Experience creating paid ad creatives that have been executed, tested, and iterated
  • Experience designing landing pages with an understanding of conversion principles
  • Comfort with basic video editing for social and web content, including reels, short-form YouTube, and simple website assets
  • Proficiency in Figma (non-negotiable), and Adobe Creative Suite including Photoshop, Illustrator, Premiere Pro, and After Effects
  • Design thinking
  • Portfolio demonstrates restraint through clean, structured, and purposeful design
  • Ability to explain the reasoning behind key design decisions
  • Designs with a specific audience in mind, not for general appeal
  • Strong understanding and consistent application of typography as a functional tool
  • Ability to produce work that appears credible to a senior B2B audience at first glance
  • Mindset: Able to work effectively from structured briefs, works at speed without compromising quality, treats design as a communication tool rather than purely creative expression, open to feedback and able to give it constructively

What We Are Not Looking For

  • Portfolios dominated by consumer brands, lifestyle work, event design, or campaigns with no B2B relevance
  • Designers who can describe visuals but cannot justify their decisions
  • Candidates without experience building a brand system
  • Designers who overcomplicate visuals with excessive fonts, colours, styles, or effects
  • Candidates who require complete creative freedom and struggle with structured briefs

Benefits

  • A growth-driven environment with opportunities to lead, innovate, and upskill continuously.

Contact: If this opportunity aligns with your career goals, kindly share your updated resume with us at careers@2basetechnologies.com.
summary: Senior Visual Designer to build brand system and marketing assets for a tech services company, focusing on B2B design with Figma and Adobe tools.

job_description:
**QA Engineer

We are seeking a detail-oriented Associate QA Engineer with 1–3 years of hands-on experience in software testing to join our QA team. In this role, you will ensure the quality and reliability of web and mobile applications by participating in test planning, test case creation, test execution, and defect tracking. You will collaborate with developers, project teams, and senior QA engineers to validate application functionality across diverse scenarios and identify potential risks.

Key Responsibilities: - Review BRD, SRS, user stories, and functional specifications to understand system requirements. - Translate requirements into structured test scenarios and documented test cases for comprehensive coverage. - Perform functional, regression, and exploratory testing for web and mobile applications across browsers, devices, and environments. - Identify edge cases, alternate flows, and boundary conditions to validate system behavior beyond standard use cases. - Log, document, and track defects with clear reproduction steps, evidence, and severity classification using defect management tools. - Validate bug fixes and perform regression testing to ensure stability during releases. - Maintain test documentation, execution reports, and QA artifacts for traceability and quality tracking. - Support test summaries, progress updates, and QA reports during development and release cycles. - Collaborate with business analysts, developers, project managers, and senior QA engineers to clarify requirements and resolve issues. - Participate in Agile/Scrum ceremonies (sprint planning, daily stand-ups, sprint reviews). - Perform basic API validation and data verification for correct system behavior at the integration level. - Own assigned modules or features to ensure testing completeness and readiness for release. - Contribute to improving test coverage, testing approaches, and QA processes. - Test AI-powered or data-driven features by validating output accuracy, response consistency, and system behavior across inputs. - Continuously learn automation fundamentals, structured testing techniques, and emerging QA tools like AI testing practices.

Required Skills & Mindset: - Strong attention to detail and structured thinking. - Willingness to deeply understand business logic before testing. - Ability to identify edge cases and risks beyond happy paths. - Analytical mindset to question unexpected system behavior. - Clear written and verbal communication for collaboration. - Strong documentation skills for defect reporting and test case writing. - Ability to work independently in a fast-paced environment. - Solid understanding of functional, regression, integration, and exploratory testing. - Experience with test case design techniques. - Familiarity with bug tracking and test management tools. - Basic exposure to API testing (Postman, REST Assured). - Understanding of Agile/Scrum methodologies and QA’s role in sprint cycles. - Familiarity with AI-assisted testing.

Good to Have: - Awareness of performance and security testing concepts. - Interest in learning test automation frameworks (Selenium, Playwright, Cypress). - Basic scripting knowledge in Python, JavaScript, or Java. - Exposure to AI-enabled applications (chatbots, recommendation engines) and testing aspects like prompt variations and edge case validation. - Awareness of modern QA practices, including continuous testing. - Ability to leverage AI-assisted tools for test case generation, data preparation, and productivity. - Curiosity about test data management and synthetic/structured test data generation. - Experience testing mobile applications or cross-platform systems, or openness to learning frameworks like Appium. - Inclination toward risk-based testing and prioritization. - Commitment to continuous learning about QA tools, methodologies, and industry practices. - ISTQB certification or equivalent testing certification.

Experience & Education: - 1–3 years of experience. - B.Tech in IT, CS, or MCA.

Contact: If this opportunity aligns with your career goals, share your updated resume at careers@2basetechnologies.com.
summary: Detail-oriented Associate QA Engineer with 1–3 years of experience in testing web and mobile applications, collaborating with cross-functional teams, and utilizing QA tools to ensure product quality.

job_description:
Business Analyst

The Business Analyst plays a key role in ensuring requirements are clearly understood, accurately documented, and traceable throughout the software development lifecycle. This role focuses on translating business and functional inputs into structured, standardized documentation that supports effective development, testing, and delivery. Working closely with business analysts, design teams, and technical stakeholders, the role ensures consistency, clarity, and alignment across all requirement artifacts.

Key Responsibilities

  • Create Software Requirement Specifications (SRS) based on inputs from Core Business Analysts, stakeholder discussions, and requirement walkthroughs
  • Prepare Business Requirement Documents (BRD) using approved templates while ensuring business intent and system behavior are clearly documented
  • Participate in requirement discussions to understand business objectives, functional flows, dependencies, and assumptions before documentation
  • Analyze requirements to identify gaps, ambiguities, and inconsistencies, and work with Core BAs to resolve them
  • Document functional flows, business rules, and acceptance criteria where applicable to support development and QA teams
  • Document wireframes, UI flows, validations, and UI specifications in collaboration with design and product teams
  • Maintain change logs, version history, and document updates throughout the project lifecycle
  • Create and manage traceability matrices linking business, functional, and technical requirements
  • Maintain cross-references across BRD, SRS, UI, API, and related documentation
  • Ensure documentation consistency, completeness, and alignment with approved scope and delivery timelines
  • Ensure accurate and timely PMS updates with structured documentation details
  • Support development, QA, UAT, and audit processes through clear, traceable, and up-to-date documentation
  • Assist in requirement reviews, walkthroughs, and validation sessions with internal and client stakeholders

Required Skills and Experience

  • 1 to 3 years of experience in Business Analysis or related roles
  • Strong experience in BRD and SRS creation
  • Hands-on experience with documentation templates, standards, and version control
  • Ability to document UI wireframes and functional flows clearly
  • Working knowledge of APIs and system integrations for documentation purposes
  • Experience with project management systems and documentation tools
  • Strong attention to detail and ability to manage multiple documents in parallel
  • Excellent written communication and stakeholder coordination skills

Minimum Qualifications

  • Bachelor’s degree in Engineering, Computer Science, Information Systems, or a related field
  • 1 to 3 years of experience as a Business Analyst or Product Analyst

Benefits

  • A growth-driven environment with opportunities to lead, innovate, and upskill continuously.

If this opportunity aligns with your career goals, kindly share your updated resume with us at careers@2basetechnologies.com
summary: The Business Analyst translates business requirements into structured documentation, collaborating with stakeholders to ensure clarity and alignment across development processes.

job_description:
Sr. DevSecOps Engineer

We are seeking a Senior DevSecOps Engineer to lead our cybersecurity, infrastructure automation, and DevSecOps practices as we transition to a secure, scalable, and modern engineering environment. This role will strengthen 2Base’s security posture, modernize pipelines, and foster a secure-by-design culture.

Key Responsibilities

  • Define, implement, and enforce security standards, policies, and best practices across cloud and on-prem environments.
  • Conduct security risk assessments, vulnerability scans, penetration testing, and execute mitigation plans.
  • Architect, maintain, and enhance secure CI/CD pipelines with automated compliance and governance checkpoints.
  • Promote a "Security First" culture across engineering, ensuring secure design and development practices.
  • Monitor, detect, investigate, and respond to security threats, incidents, breaches, and anomalies.
  • Develop Infrastructure-as-Code practices using Terraform, Ansible, and other automation frameworks.
  • Design secure cloud architectures across AWS, Azure, or GCP for scalable, compliant deployments.
  • Build and secure containerized environments using Docker and Kubernetes with enforced security baselines.
  • Implement secure monitoring, logging, SIEM, and alerting systems for infrastructure and application layers.
  • Optimize system reliability, scalability, performance, and fault tolerance across critical workloads.
  • Mentor DevOps engineers in secure development, automation, and operational excellence.
  • Lead technical strategy, tool selection, process improvements, and DevSecOps roadmap execution.
  • Collaborate with BA, PM, and Engineering teams to align security, performance, and delivery goals.

Required Skills & Experience

  • 4–8 years of experience in DevOps, Cloud, or Infrastructure engineering with strong security exposure.
  • Hands-on experience with CI/CD tools (Jenkins, GitLab CI, GitHub Actions).
  • Expertise in AWS, Azure, or GCP cloud platforms with a focus on secure architecture.
  • Proficiency in Infrastructure-as-Code (Terraform, Ansible).
  • Experience securing Docker and Kubernetes environments.
  • Strong understanding of networking technologies (LAN/WAN, firewalls, VPN, load balancers).
  • Knowledge of security frameworks (OWASP, NIST, CIS Benchmarks).
  • Experience with monitoring/logging tools (Prometheus, Grafana, ELK, CloudWatch, SIEM).
  • Scripting skills (Python, Bash, Shell).
  • Excellent troubleshooting, analytical, and problem-solving skills.
  • Strong communication and collaboration skills to influence engineering teams.

Benefits

  • A growth-driven environment with opportunities to lead, innovate, and upskill continuously.

Contact: Share your resume at careers@2basetechnologies.com.

At 2Base, we are rebuilding the Business Analyst function into a world-class, product-minded, clarity-driven team. This role is not a documentation role, screen updater role, or support role. job_description:
Senior UI/UX Designer

We are seeking a Senior UI/UX Designer to create intuitive, visually engaging, and user-friendly designs for web applications, mobile applications, and digital platforms. The designer will collaborate with Business Analysts, Developers, and Project Stakeholders to translate functional requirements and wireframes into high-quality UI designs and interactive prototypes. The role demands strong design thinking, attention to detail, and the ability to deliver consistent and modern user experiences across platforms.

Key Responsibilities

Note: Advanced backend development is not required.
